shape.onResolved: function(location) — invoked with the resolved-location object { lat, lon, label, source } once a location is established
shape.onStatus: function(statusString) — invoked with progress/status text the shell can display (e.g. 'Detecting your location...', 'Location access denied — enter a location below')

shape.renderInto: function(containerElement) — section-1 mounts its location UI (manual-entry control and status) into the supplied container

notes: section-4 (app shell) owns the page and the overall flow. It gives section-1 a container to render its location UI into and receives the resolved location via the onResolved callback, then drives section-2. section-1 owns all geolocation/geocoding logic and its own UI; the shell only orchestrates. The shell must not assume location is resolved synchronously — it is delivered via callback.
